skill.name: mdr-745-specialist
skill.description: EU MDR 2017/745 support for medical device compliance planning, classification, technical documentation, clinical evidence, UDI, PMS, and notified body preparation.
skill.summary: Assess EU MDR 2017/745 obligations and organize medical device compliance work.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed in context and found to be false positives. The flagged weak cryptography terms are MDR regulatory text, the C2 hit is a conformity assessment module label, and the backtick hits are Markdown formatting rather than shell execution.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: Static weak cryptography alerts appear to match regulatory text such as MDR and related documentation labels. No evidence found of cryptographic operations, hash functions, credential handling, or security-sensitive algorithm selection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain medical device regulatory content, placeholder asset labels, or documentation structure. I did not find cryptographic API calls or instructions to use weak algorithms.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Markdown Backtick Execution Matches
low_findings.description: Static Ruby or shell backtick execution alerts are Markdown formatting around a documentation tree and resource names. No evidence found of executable Ruby code, shell command interpolation, or user-controlled command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are inside Markdown code fences or inline file-name formatting. They are not program source lines and contain no shell execution semantics.

low_findings.title: Dismissed C2 Keyword Match
low_findings.description: The C2 alert refers to Module C2 in an EU MDR conformity assessment route. This is regulatory terminology, not command-and-control behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding section discusses MDR classification and conformity assessment routes. There is no network endpoint, persistence logic, beaconing, or remote-control behavior.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Network Reconnaissance Match
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance alert appears in a notified body selection checklist discussing geographic scope and market access. No evidence found of scanning, probing, host discovery, or network enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is ordinary business guidance for choosing a notified body. It does not reference network targets, sockets, ports, scanners, or discovery commands.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Evidence Found
low_findings.description: The reviewed files do not contain text that claims higher authority, asks the evaluator to ignore instructions, or attempts to override the audit process.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The main skill file contains domain guidance and resource lists. I did not find suspicious override phrases or instructions directed at the security evaluator.

content.value_statement: Medical device teams need structured help interpreting EU MDR 2017/745 obligations. This skill organizes classification, evidence, documentation, UDI, PMS, and notified body preparation into practical workflows.
actual_capabilities: Guides device classification using MDR Annex VIII concepts.
Maps technical documentation needs to Annex II and Annex III topics.
Outlines clinical evidence, PMCF, and clinical evaluation planning.
Structures UDI, EUDAMED, and economic operator compliance tasks.
Supports gap analysis and transition planning for existing device files.
Prepares checklists for notified body readiness and audit preparation.
limitations: Does not replace legal advice from qualified regulatory counsel.
Does not submit documents to EUDAMED or notified bodies.
Does not verify current guidance without user-provided source material.
Does not make final regulatory decisions for manufacturers.

use_cases.title: Classify a New Device
use_cases.description: Review device characteristics and produce a reasoned MDR classification path with open questions.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Technical File Gap Review
use_cases.description: Compare available documentation against MDR technical documentation expectations and prioritize missing evidence.

use_cases.title: Plan PMS and PMCF Activities
use_cases.description: Draft a surveillance planning outline that connects clinical evidence, PSUR, PMCF, and incident reporting tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Basic MDR Orientation
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a concise compliance overview.
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain the main EU MDR 2017/745 obligations for this device type: [device]. Keep the answer practical and list unknowns.

prompt_templates.title: Classification Rationale
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess the likely MDR classification for [device]. Consider intended purpose, duration, invasiveness, software role, and clinical use.

prompt_templates.title: Technical Documentation Gap Analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Structured review of an existing device file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this technical documentation inventory against MDR Annex II and III topics. Identify gaps, evidence needs, and priority actions: [inventory].

prompt_templates.title: Integrated MDR Readiness Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning across regulatory, quality, and clinical workstreams.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an MDR readiness plan for [device portfolio]. Include classification, clinical evidence, UDI, PMS, notified body preparation, risks, owners, and milestones.

output_examples.input: Assess whether a reusable surgical instrument may need notified body involvement.
output_examples.output: A concise classification discussion, key facts to confirm, likely conformity assessment considerations, and a list of documents to prepare.

output_examples.input: Review a technical file inventory for MDR gaps.
output: A prioritized gap list by documentation area.
Suggested owner and next review step.

output_examples.input: Create a PMS planning outline for a Class IIb device.
output_examples.output: A PMS and PMCF outline covering data sources, reporting cadence, PSUR inputs, trend monitoring, and escalation triggers.

best_practices: Provide device intended purpose, patient population, duration, invasiveness, and software role before asking for classification.
Attach current guidance, notified body feedback, or internal procedures when asking for detailed interpretation.
Treat outputs as planning support and route final decisions through qualified regulatory review.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a final legal determination without supplying device facts and source documents.
Do not use generated checklists as released quality records without formal review.
Do not assume older MDD evidence satisfies MDR requirements without a documented gap assessment.

faq.question: Can this skill classify my medical device?
faq.answer: It can help develop a classification rationale and identify missing facts. The manufacturer remains responsible for the final classification decision.

faq.question: Does it provide legal advice?
faq.answer: No. It provides regulatory planning support and should be reviewed by qualified regulatory or legal professionals.

faq.question: Can it prepare technical documentation?
faq.answer: It can outline MDR documentation needs, create checklists, and review inventories. It does not certify document completeness.

faq.question: Can it help with clinical evidence planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It can structure clinical evaluation, literature, clinical investigation, and PMCF planning questions.

faq.question: Does it connect to EUDAMED?
faq.answer: No. It can explain UDI and EUDAMED preparation tasks, but it does not submit or retrieve registry data.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide intended purpose, device description, risk class assumptions, evidence inventory, markets, and any notified body feedback.
